items.back: Apply within designated Class C airspace AND the associated outer area. Designed to keep ATC informed of all aircraft operating within Class C airspace. Sequenced, separated, and provided safety alerts/advisories.
1. Sequencing of all aircraft to the primary airport. 2. Standard IFR services to IFR aircraft. 3. Traffic advisories and safety alerts between all aircraft.
Establish two-way radio communications BEFORE entering Class C. If controller responds with call sign, communication is established. Differs from Class B which requires explicit ATC clearance.

VFR helicopters/powered-lift need NOT be separated from IFR helicopters/powered-lift. Traffic info and safety alerts still required. VFR aircraft at or below 2,500 ft AGL within 5 NM may operate without regard to Class C procedures during SVFR at satellite airports.
